title: Virtual line registration system
abstract: An improved core network that allows a UE to still initiate and/or receive communications via a native SIM line even if the registration of a virtual line fails is described herein. For example, the UE may include embedded virtual line settings that, when enabled, cause the UE to transmit a virtual line registration request to the core network. The core network can determine whether the UE is authorized to register a virtual line. If the UE is authorized, the core network can register a native SIM line, register the virtual line, and transmit a single message to the UE indicating that the native SIM and virtual lines are registered. If the UE is not authorized, the core network still registers the native SIM line and transmits a single message to the UE indicating that the native SIM line is registered.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method as implemented by one or more computing devices in a core network of a network operator, the one or more computing devices configured with specific computer-executable instructions comprising: receiving, from a user equipment (UE), a request to register a virtual line with the core network: initiating a deregistration of a native SIM line associated with the UE; determining that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line; initiating a registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; receiving an indication that the registration of the native SIM line is successful; and transmitting a message to the UE indicating that the native SIM line is registered, wherein reception of the message causes the UE to indicate that the virtual line is not registered and causes the UE to be capable of initiating and receiving communications over the core network using the native SIM line. 2. The computer-implemented method of Claim 1, wherein the request to register the virtual line comprises a service authorization token (SAT). 3. The computer-implemented method of Claim 2, wherein determining that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line further comprises: querying an A AG using the SAT; receiving a response indicating that the SAT is valid; querying an identity and access management (IAM) using an identifier associated with the UE; and receiving a second response indicating that the UE is not associated with permissions that allow the UE to register the virtual line. 4. The computer-implemented method of Claim 2, wherein determining that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line further comprises: querying an A AG using the SAT; and receiving a response indicating that the SAT is invalid. 5. The computer- implemented method of Claim 1, wherein initiating a registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE further comprises initiating the registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE prior to determining that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line. 6. The computer-implemented method of Claim 1, wherein initiating a registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE further comprises initiating the registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE after determining that the LIE is not authorized to register the virtual line. 7. The computer-implemented method of Claim 1 , further comprising: receiving, from the UE, a second request to register a second virtual line with the core network: initiating a second deregistration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; determining that the UE is authorized to register the second virtual line: initiating a second registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; receiving an indication that the second registration of the native SIM line is successful; initiating a registration of the second virtual line; and transmitting a second message to the UE indicating that the native SIM line and the second virtual line are registered, wherein reception of the second message causes the UE to indicate that the second virtual line is registered. 8. The computer-implemented method of Claim 7, wherein the second request to register the second virtual line comprises a SAT, and wherein determining that the UE, is authorized to register the second virtual line further comprises: querying an AAG using the SAT; receiving a response indicating that the SAT is valid; querying an identity and access management (I AM) using an identifier associated with the UE; and receiving a second response indicating that the UE is associated with permissions that allow the UE to register the second virtual line. 9. The computer-implemented method of Claim 8, wherein the response comprises a first session information protocol (SIP) 200OK message, and wherein the second response comprises a second SIP 2QQOK message. 10. The computer-implemented method of Claim 9, wherein the second message comprises a session initiation protocol (SIP) 200OK message. 11. Non-transitory, computer-readable storage media comprising computer-executable instructions, wherein the computer-executable instructions, when executed by a computer system, cause the computer system to: receive, from a user equipment (UE), a request to register a virtual line with a core network; initiate a deregistration of a native SIM line associated with the UE; determine that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line; initiate, in response to the determination that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line, a registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; receive an indication that the registration of the native SIM line is successful; and transmit a message to the UE indicating that the native SIM line is registered such that the UE can communicate over the core network using the native SIM line. 12. The non-transitory, computer-readable storage media of Claim 11, wherein the request to register the virtual line comprises a sendee authorization token (SAT). 13. The non-transitory, computer-readable storage media of Claim 12, wherein the computer-executable instructions further cause the computer system to: query an A AG using the SAT; receive a response indicating that the SAT is valid; query an identity and access management (IAM) using an identifier associated with the UE; and receive a second response indicating that the UE is not associated with permissions that allow the UE to register the virtual line. 14. The non-transitory, computer-readable storage media of Claim 12, wherein the computer-executable instructions further cause the computer system to: query an AAG using the SAT; and 

 receive a response indicating that the SAT is invalid. 15. The non-transitory, computer-readable storage media of Claim 11, wherein the computer-executable instructions further cause the computer system to: receive, from the UE, a second request to register a second virtual line with the core network; initiate a second deregistration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; determine that the UE is authorized to register the second virtual line; initiate a second registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; receive an indication that the second registration of the native SIM line is successful; initiate a registration of the second virtual line; and transmit a second message to the UE indicating that the native SIM line and the second virtual line are registered, wherein reception of the second message causes the UE to indicate that the second virtual line is registered. 16. The non-transitory, computer-readable storage media of Claim 15, wherein the second message comprises a hypertext transfer protocol (SIP) 200OK message. 17. A core network comprising: an interrogating call session control function (I-CSCF); and a proxy-call session control function (P-CSCF) comprising a processor in communication with the I-CSCF and configured with specific computer-executable instructions to: receive, from a user equipment (UE), a request to register a virtual line with a core network; initiate a deregistration of a native SIM line associated with the UE; initiate a registration of the native SIM line associated with the UE; receive an indication that the registration of the native SIM line is successful; determine that the UE is not authorized to register the virtual line; and transmit a message to the UE indicating that the native SIM line is registered such that the UE can communicate over the core network using the native SIM line. 18. The core network of Claim 17, wirerein the request to register the virtual line comprises a service authorization token (SAT). 19. The core network of Claim 18, wherein the P-CSCF is further configured with specific computer-executable instructions to: query an AAG using the SAT; receive a response indicating that the SAT is valid; query an identity and access management (IAM) using an identifier associated with the UE; and receive a second response indicating that the UE is not associated with permissions that allow r the UE to register the virtual line. 20. The core network of Claim 18, wherein the P-CSCF is further configured with specific computer-executable instructions to: query an AAG using the SAT; and receive a response indicating that the SAT is invalid.
